mnemonics: PLEASE KEEP SYSTEMS MODULAR - Principles (Layered, Event-driven), Kernel (Monolithic, Micro), Structures (Simple, Virtual Machines), Models (Client-Server, Modular)
keyPoints: Operating System Services provide user and system functions like UI, program execution, I/O, file manipulation, communications, error detection, and resource allocation.

System Calls are the programming interface to OS services, often accessed via APIs. Common categories include process control, file manipulation, device manipulation, information maintenance, and communications.
Operating System Structures define design approaches, including Simple (MS-DOS), Layered, Microkernel (Mach), Modules, and Virtual Machines (VMware).
commonMistakes: Confusing the kernel (core OS component) with the shell (user interface). The kernel manages resources, while the shell is the command interpreter.
Assuming system calls and regular function calls are the same. System calls involve a context switch to kernel mode and are more expensive.
